items.title: US imposes a 25 percent tariff on some Brazilian imports, citing unfair trade practices
items.description: The United States will slap 25 percent tariffs on some Brazilian goods starting July 22 following a yearlong investigation under the Trade Act of 1974. The probe concluded that Brazil engaged in unfair trade practices. Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva condemned the move, saying it was politically motivated to favour his rival for upcoming elections, Flavio Bolsonaro. Also in this edition, we look at the Japanese firm trying to bring back 1980's style corporate culture.

He has spoken with FRANCE 24 multiple times to share some of the still photographs he has taken in Ukraine – both in major cities like Kyiv and from the frontline. items.title: Toronto: Air ranked among world's worst as wildfire smoke chokes city
items.description: Toronto’s skies have turned a hazy yellow as smoke blown in from wildfires blankets Canada’s largest city and sends pollution levels soaring. On Wednesday, Toronto had the worst air quality of any major city in the world, according to Swiss firm IQAir, edging out New Delhi and Kinshasa.

items.title: Po river choked by algae bloom as Italy battles heat and navigability woes
items.description: Italy is stepping up efforts to clear thick algae from the River Po in Turin after weeks of high temperatures accelerated its spread along the country’s longest river.

items.title: Trump Media unveils data feed for businesses tracking Truth Social posts
Powerful storm floods hundreds of homes in Russia’s urals
items.description: Powerful storm floods hundreds of homes in Russia's urals

items.title: Irrepressible Argentina produce the goods in epic win over England
items.description: While the Albiceleste have celebrated epic knockout wins plenty of times in the recent past, few would have tasted sweeter than this most recent success. Leer más

items.title: Shettima’s final test, By Azu Ishiekwene
items.description: Vice President Kashim Shettima cannot be blamed for having doubts about whether President Bola Ahmed Tinubu would renominate him as his running mate for a second term. As governor of Lagos State for eight years, Tinubu used three deputies: Kofoworola Bucknor-Akerele, Femi Pedro, and Abiodun Ogunleye. Only Senate President Godswill Akpabio (as Akwa Ibom governor) […] The post Shettima’s final test, By Azu Ishiekwene appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: What's behind the Iran regime's 'revenge propaganda'?
items.description: Calls for revenge over the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei are growing louder in Iran. Through increasingly harsh rhetoric, the leadership is seeking to mobilize supporters at home while warning opponents abroad.

items.title: Italy: melting ice statues protest heatwave conditions in Rome
items.description: Greenpeace Italy and the CGIL union melted ice statues outside Rome's Colosseum on 15 July 2026. The protest highlighted the impact of heatwaves on outdoor workers and called for a fossil fuel phase-out.

items.title: EU court endorses Spanish PM's contentious Catalan amnesty
items.description: The EU's top court has backed a Spanish law giving amnesty to Catalan separatists over the independence referendum. The deal let Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez stay in office in 2023 and prompted major opposition protests.

items.title: Uganda opposition leader refuses court-appointed lawyers at treason trial
items.description: Ugandan opposition leader Kizza Besigye refused to accept the lawyers being forced upon him for his treason trial on Wednesday after his chosen advocates were arrested or barred from the country.

items.title: At least 11 dead in Algiers orphanage fire
items.description: A fire tore through an orphanage on the outskirts of Algiers early Thursday, killing at least 11 people and injuring 19 others, Algerian civil defence officials said.

items.title: France arrests arson suspects amid Fontainebleau forest fire
items.description: Firefighters are attempting to tackle a major fire burning since Sunday in the much-visited Fontainebleau forest, near France's capital Paris. Two people have been arrested for suspected arson.

items.title: West Asia War updates: In essential and existential war with America, says Iran's top negotiator
items.description: "If Iran is not going to benefit from the memorandum of understanding, we have no reason to adhere to it," says Iran's top negotiator Qalibaf

items.title: Hong Kong official warns booksellers on security risks after raids
items.description: The police operation marks the third round of arrests targeting independent bookstores in four months.

items.title: World Cup culture wars and the legend of Lionel Messi
items.description: World Cups don’t occur in a vacuum. Rather, they are an integral part of the construction of cultural subjectivity across the globe given the prevalence and capillarity of football. Argentina’s central role in global football and its obsessed population add complexity and intensity. Leer más

items.title: The political risks from war in Iran. And, ICE's use of force is rising, report finds
items.description: The political implications the war in Iran is having on the GOP. And, a new report has found that ICE officers' use of force is not rare since President Trump took office for his second term.

items.title: Dramatic video shows wildfire surround freight train in Canada
items.description: CN Rail told the BBC that operations were temporarily suspended in the affected area in the province of Ontario.

items.title: Brazil's Lula blasts Flavio Bolsonaro for requesting U.S. tariff delay
items.description: The delay would prevent the rate hike from being interpreted "as an attempt to influence the outcome" of the elections, Lula’s right-wing rival said.

items.title: Nigeria issues flood alert for 14 states as rainy season intensifies
items.description: Fourteen of Nigeria’s 36 states were put on flood alert on Wednesday as authorities warned of the risk of moderate flooding over the next few days as the rainy season intensifies.

items.title: Senegal government silences Football Federation amid World Cup row
items.description: As tensions are escalating following Senegal's exit from the World Cup, the Senegalese government has stepped in and ordered the country's Football Federation to refrain from giving interviews or making public statements until further notice.

items.title: Zimbabwe says nearly 100,000 of its citizens have left South Africa
items.description: It says it has expanded its repatriation and reintegration efforts as the number of Zimbabeans requiring assistance continues to rise.

items.title: As Milei’s aura fades, Argentina starts to look for a third way
items.description: With President Milei’s popularity at its lowest level in more than two years, talk of the 2027 election has taken over investor trips and political circles. The question being asked is whether it might be the moment for a third way. Leer más

items.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
items.description: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

items.title: One dead, 24 missing after boat sinks in Indonesia: rescuers
items.description: An engine failure caused the boat to sink as it sailed near Selayar

items.title: Argentina inflation slows to lowest level since last August
items.description: Inflation slowed for a third straight month to the lowest level since August in a victory for President Javier Milei. Leer más

items.title: Chipmakers chip away at stocks, oil hovers at $85
Family fixes Friday for Umahi aide’s burial despite police insistence on autopsy
items.description: According to the police spokesperson, Joshua Ukandu, the family has opposed plans to conduct a post mortem examination. The post Family fixes Friday for Umahi aide’s burial despite police insistence on autopsy appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Crackdown on 'objectionable' books in Indian-administered Kashmir sparks row
items.description: Authorities in Indian-administered Kashmir have asked educational institutions to review every book on their premises.

items.title: EU court upholds Spanish amnesty law as compatible with EU law
items.description: Amnesty for people involved in Catalonia independence movement introduced in 2024 by PM Pedro Sanchez's government.

items.title: Joy in Nigeria's Oyo State as abducted students reunite with relatives
items.description: Scenes of joy erupted in Nigeria's Oyo State on Wednesday, as rescued students reunited with their relatives after months in captivity.

items.title: Zelenskyy fires Ukraine's tech-savvy defense minister in government reshuffle
items.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired the country's popular defense minister, who pushed for innovation in the battlefield through the use of drones and turned the tables on Russia.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Indian national stabbed in U.S. over his religion: reports
items.description: Syed Sohailuddin, a worker in the Valley City Mall in West Valley City, Utah, was allegedly stabbed by Peter Michael Larsen, who asked the victim his religion, Fox 13 News reported

items.title: Why America's hospitals keep looking to India for nurses
items.description: Thousands of Indian nurses aspire to work in the US, navigating yearslong visa backlogs to fill a growing healthcare workforce shortage.

items.title: British Embassy playfully defuses Argentina-England match tensions
items.description: Embassy's Instagram account shares a spoof "internal memo" instructing its community manager on how to behave – win or lose – in the wake of Wednesday’s World Cup semi-final. Leer más

items.title: Moment US Navy jet stuns packed Florida beach with low flypast
items.description: A US Navy Blue Angels jet sent some beach items up in the air as it flew at a low altitude over a crowd in Pensacola Beach, Florida.

items.title: US hosts global meet on ‘far-left terror’: Who’s attending, why it matters
items.description: More than 65 countries expected to address 'renewed threats' from 'far-left political violence and terrorism'.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: India's Wipro lags peers after earnings miss, weak forecast
Sierra Leone drops treason charges against ex-president Koroma
items.description: Sierra Leone has dropped treason charges against former president Ernest Bai Koroma, officials said Tuesday, for his alleged role in what authorities called an attempted 2023 coup.

items.title: Gibraltar enters Schengen as border fence comes down under EU-UK treaty
items.description: Spain and the British territory of Gibraltar removed their border fence on 15 July 2026 under a landmark EU-UK treaty. Residents celebrated as passport checks moved to the airport and port.

items.title: Gautam Adani denies any deal linked to dismissal of U.S. criminal case in sworn affidavit
items.description: Addressing speculation over Adani Group's proposed U.S. investment plans, the businessman said the group's intention to invest $10 billion had been publicly announced on November 13, 2024, before the indictment was unsealed

items.title: The crews stuck at sea as the Strait of Hormuz crisis grows
items.description: The Strait of Hormuz crisis is leaving thousands of crew members on commercial ships stranded. Arsenio Dominguez, head of the UN's International Maritime Organization, explains.

items.title: Former Aso Villa chaplain, William Okoye, is dead, CAN mourns
items.description: A statement on behalf of the Board of Trustees and the National Executive Council of the All Christians Fellowship Mission said Mr Okoye died peacefully on Wednesday, 15 July. The post Former Aso Villa chaplain, William Okoye, is dead, CAN mourns appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Alcatraz boat capsizing in San Francisco leaves 4 dead
items.description: A three-deck pontoon vessel sank off the famous prison island, which is now a tourist destination. Three people who were missing are presumed dead after a search-and-rescue operation was called off.

items.title: Messi ventures into uncharted waters with Argentina-England semi-final clash
items.description: Every major footballing nation has fallen beneath his blade. All but one: England, the giant Lionel Messi will face for the first time on Wednesday in the semi-finals of the 2026 World Cup. Leer más

items.title: Ahead of French PM's visit, Rabat faces allegations of spying on Paris
items.description: French Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu is set to visit Morocco on July 16 in an effort to strengthen ties between Paris and Rabat, whether it is for trade or defence. But as the visit starts, an investigation has revealed that Morocco has used Israeli spyware to spy on several French ministers, including the French prime minister himself. FRANCE 24's Shirli Sitbon tells us more.

items.title: Argentina come from behind to beat England 2-1
items.description: The defending champions strike again. Led 1-0 after Anthony Gordon opened the score for England, Argentina kept believing and turned the game on its head. Guided by Lionel Messi, Lionel Scaloni's men sealed a 2-1 victory after an injury-time winner saw them book a spot against Spain in the final. England manager Thomas Tuchel opted to defend after taking the lead, a choice that backfired as the Three Lions' time in the United States comes to an end.

items.title: Germany: Gender switch neo-Nazi sent to men's prison
items.description: Far-right provocateur Marla Svenja Liebich was transferred to a men's prison after being extradited from the Czech Republic. Authorities say the decision took into account the safety of prisoners at a women's facility.

items.title: New monkey species with orange lips found 'hiding' in DR Congo forest
items.description: Scientists describe "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.

items.title: U.K. urges FIFA to investigate Argentina over Falklands banner at World Cup
items.description: On July 15, Argentina beat England 2-1 in a World Cup semifinal; during post-match celebrations, Argentine players held a banner handed over by fans in the stands, reading ‘Las Malvinas son Argentinas’— ‘The Malvinas are Argentine.’

items.title: Celebrations as Gibraltar-Spain border fence removed
items.description: The symbolic midnight ceremony was followed by large crowds crossing between Gibraltar and Spain without border checks.
